business.headline: Your flat is delayed. Your EMI isn't. What now?You're paying the EMI. The builder missed the deadline. With over 5 lakh homes stuck across India, delayed possession has become a financial nightmare for thousands of buyers. Here's what experts say you can do when your dream home doesn't arrive on time.

Rosneft CEO predicts India will drive 50% of global oil demand growth by 2035Rosneft CEO cited International Energy Agency estimates projecting that India's oil consumption would reach nearly eight million barrels per day by 2035, representing growth of 44 per cent, while global demand overall would increase by about five per cent.

Indians have no will for a WILLA house. A few bank accounts. Some investments. Perhaps a family business. For many Indians, these assets represent decades of hard work. Yet surprisingly few leave behind clear instructions on who gets what, creating uncertainty for the very people they wanted to protect.

Oil India reports second gas discovery in Andaman offshore blockOil India has reported natural gas in the Vijayapuram-3 exploratory well in the Andaman offshore basin. The find adds to evidence of hydrocarbon potential in the block as appraisal work continues.

India-US trade deal's first tranche likely by mid-July, says Piyush GoyalSpeaking about the discussions, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said the meetings held between June 2 and June 4 were very encouraging.

India's economy grows 7.7% in FY26 despite global turmoil, war concernsProvisional government estimates show economic growth accelerated from the previous year, with robust expansion in the January-March quarter helping lift full-year GDP growth to 7.7%.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as RBI holds rates; rupee logs biggest gain since AprilMarkets ended lower after the RBI kept the repo rate at 5.25%, while the rupee posted its biggest one-day gain since April. Investors are now weighing inflation, crude prices, foreign outflows and West Asia tensions for clues on market direction.

Gold, FDs or real estate? I asked experts where to invest during rupee weaknessWhen the rupee comes under pressure, many investors start looking for a safe haven. But which asset actually delivers—gold, FDs or real estate? I spoke to experts to find out.

Before Sebi stepped in, Rajesh Exports had already raised eyebrows for yearsSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports has brought fresh scrutiny to its reported revenues and subsidiary disclosures. The action has revived years of public questions over how the company matched vast turnover with thin profits.

The hidden inflation nobody is talking about: Transport inflationAs transportation costs increase across the supply chain, businesses eventually pass those higher expenses on to consumers. The result is 'invisible inflation' – a force that pushes up the cost of almost everything but is rarely talked about on its own.

RBI MPC meeting: 7 key takeaways on repo rate, inflation, growth and moreThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced its latest monetary policy decisions, keeping the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and maintaining a neutral stance. Here are seven key takeaways from the RBI's June MPC meeting.

Rajesh Exports shares hit 5% lower circuit for 2nd straight day after Sebi orderRajesh Exports shares hit the 5% lower circuit for a second straight session after Sebi's interim order. The sell-off reflects investor caution as the regulator examines alleged revenue inflation and orders a fresh forensic audit.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: What it means for your home loan and EMIFor millions of home loan borrowers, the RBI's repo rate decision is closely watched because it can influence borrowing costs. With the central bank keeping the repo rate steady at 5.25%, should you expect any change in your monthly EMI?

RBI rolls out multiple measures to attract more dollars amid rupee weaknessThe RBI has unveiled a package of bond, swap and deposit measures to draw more dollars into India while keeping the repo rate unchanged. The steps are aimed at easing foreign currency liquidity strains as oil prices rise and investor outflows weigh on the rupee.

Higher crude prices forcing a rethink on inflation and growth outlook, says RBIThe RBI kept the repo rate unchanged as Sanjay Malhotra warned that the West Asia conflict is lifting oil prices and disrupting supplies. The central bank has cut its growth forecast and raised its inflation outlook as cost pressures begin to build.

Centre exempts foreign investors from capital gains tax on govt bondsThe tax break is aimed at boosting foreign investment in government bonds, improving post-tax returns for overseas investors and helping attract more stable capital flows into India.

RBI keeps repo rate steady at 5.25%, reduces GDP growth projection for FY27All six members of the MPC voted unanimously to maintain the policy repo rate at 5.25%. The central bank also retained its "neutral" policy stance, signalling that future decisions will depend on incoming economic data and evolving global developments.

PwC review flags accounting irregularities in IndusInd Bank treasury operationsA confidential PwC review reportedly found that manual treasury entries at IndusInd Bank offset trading losses and created a Rs 2,201.76 crore receivable pool. The findings have raised fresh questions over hedging links, accounting treatment and controls around manual adjustments.

Markets open higher as investors await RBI rate decision; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 191.96 points, or 0.26%, to 74,551.97 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 39 points, or 0.17%, to 23,455.55.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall or rise on RBI MPC decision?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,565.00 at 8:17 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 could open above its previous close of 23,416.55.

entertainment.headline: Director Nag Ashwin shares new update on Kalki 2 shoot, promises rapid pace Filmmaker Nag Ashwin has shared a major update on the Kalki 2898 AD sequel. With filming set to resume soon, new details have emerged about the cast, story and production plans.

Shaktiman casting row: Actor Lalit Parimoo reacts to Mukesh Khanna rejecting Ranveer Lalit Parimoo has reacted to Mukesh Khanna's continued opposition to Ranveer Singh playing Shaktimaan. He said that publicity-driven narratives are common in the entertainment industry.

Easy to blame the actress: Janhvi Kapoor's make-up artist weighs in on Peddi row The debate around Janhvi Kapoor's role in Peddi has intensified after a member of her team shared claims about what happened behind the scenes. Here's what sparked the latest discussion.

Jr NTR's first baking attempt for two little fans wins hearts online Jr NTR won hearts after sharing a video of himself baking cookies for two young fans and personally having them delivered to them. The thoughtful gesture quickly went viral, with fans praising the actor's kindness.

Ranveer Singh's father thanked Poonam Dhillon for backing him amid Don 3 row A conversation between Ranveer Singh's father and Poonam Dhillon has shed new light on the Don 3 controversy. Here's what was said after the FWICE directive was withdrawn.

He kept kissing me: Actor Priya Bapat says she was harassed on set, felt helpless Actor Priya Bapat recalled a co-actor repeatedly kissing her while improvising during a song sequence in her debut film. She said the experience left her distressed.

Hai Jawani Toh... box office Day 2: Varun film trails Border 2 after modest start Varun Dhawan, Mrunal Thakur and Pooja Hegde's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai earned Rs 7.25 crore on its second day. The David Dhawan comedy remains behind Peddi and Varun Dhawan's earlier release Border 2.

Bandar box office Day 2: Bobby Deol film banks on word of mouth, earns Rs 1 crore Bobby Deol and Anurag Kashyap's Bandar posed a strong rise on its second day after a modest opening. It earned Rs 1.45 in two days.

Peddi box office Day 3: Ram Charan film nears Rs 250 crore globally amid backlash Ram Charan's Peddi has crossed a major box-office milestone within just three days of release. Here's how much the film earned on Day 3 and what its latest numbers reveal.

Dua Lipa reportedly makes £5,000 peace offering to locals after Palermo backlash Dua Lipa's lavish Sicily wedding reportedly left some Palermo residents unhappy. A reported 5,000 euros gesture has now added a fresh twist to the celebrity wedding everyone is talking about.

PM Modi mourns Salim Kumar's death, remembers actor's remarkable legacy PM Narendra Modi has joined tributes pouring in for Malayalam actor Salim Kumar. Here's what the Prime Minister said while remembering the National Award-winning star.

Why Ram Charan's Peddi is radical for Telugu cinema and where it falls short Ram Charan's Peddi is the kind of ambitious, larger-than-life film Telugu cinema rarely entrusts to a star at the peak of his popularity. While the actor delivers a committed performance, the film's writing fails to do justice to the risk he took.

Peddi row: Ratna Pathak's take on privileged women and objectification goes viral Amid the ongoing Peddi controversy over the portrayal of Janhvi Kapoor's character, an old video of Ratna Pathak discussing objectification in cinema has resurfaced and gone viral.

Aishwarya Rai, Akshaye Khanna's Taal dance clip goes viral after 25 years. Watch A 1999 video of Aishwarya Rai and Akshaye Khanna performing to Taal Se Taal Mila in Miami has resurfaced online and gone viral.

Madhoo recalls Nana Patekar's unexpected slap on Yeshwant sets: I hit him back Madhoo Shah recalled an intense moment on the sets of Yeshwant, revealing that she slapped Nana Patekar back after he unexpectedly hit her during an emotional scene.

Sunny Deol showers love on Bobby for Bandar success; shares pic with mother Prakash Sunny Deol shared a rare photograph of Bobby Deol with their mother Prakash Kaur after Bandar won praise. The post highlighted the family's pride as Bobby's performance drew wide appreciation.

Did Janhvi Kapoor flag concerns about her Peddi role? Leaked chats go viral Alleged leaked chats circulated by Janhvi Kapoor's fan clubs suggest that the actor had raised concerns about camera angles and the portrayal of her character in Peddi long before the film came under public scrutiny.

Shilpa Shinde reacts to criticism over recent remarks, says do whatever you can Shilpa Shinde has shared cryptic social media posts amid backlash over her admission that she made a false sexual harassment allegation during her Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain! dispute.

Chinese actor Jin Ze dies at 33; agency appeals for privacy, warns against rumours Actor Jin Ze, known for popular Chinese romance dramas such as Begin Again and Legally Romance, died at the age of 33.

general news.headline: Delhi University professor refused to sell Bengal house, tenant couple killed herPolice have arrested a couple for murdering Delhi University professor Debosmita Paul after she refused to sell them her property in Bengal's Bardhaman, with investigators saying the couple executed the premeditated crime after travelling nearly 1,400 km to Delhi with their child to avoid suspicion.

We went to cockroach party's Jantar Mantar protest. This is what we sawHundreds of "cockroaches" packed Jantar Mantar to demand Education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Cockroach Janta Party founder, Abhijeet Dipke, got them to Jantar Mantar. But it was educator-activist Sonam Wangchuk who turned a scattered gathering into an attentive audience.

Law student dies, woman collapses at Mumbai concert; alcohol, drug angle under lensWhile investigators suspect excessive alcohol consumption may have contributed to the incident, police have not ruled out a possible drug angle. The exact cause of death will be determined only after the post-mortem examination.

BCCI requests Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents to travel with Team IndiaThe Indian Cricket Board has made special provisions for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on tours of Sri Lanka and UK. The BCCI has asked Vaibhav's parents to travel with Team India as the 15-year-old assimilates into the national setup.

How Bengal's Bapuji cake is still popular with GenZ and millennials alikeTrends may come and go, but Bapuji Cake's foundation remains unchanged. As the bakery's owner Amitava Jana said during an exclusive conversation, the beloved brand continues to be guided by three enduring values — humanity, loyalty and ethics.

HYDRAA inspired by Hitler: Revanth Reddy's remark on encroachment drive triggers rowSpeaking on Saturday at a conclave in Bengaluru, Reddy asserted that the Hyderabad Disaster Response and Asset Protection Agency (HYDRAA) drew its name and concept from the German dictator's alleged fondness for the word 'Hydra.'

Shreyas Iyer's bold first words as India captain: Won't be under anyone's shadowShreyas Iyer has embraced the challenge of becoming India's new T20I captain, but insists the leadership role will not change who he is. The Mumbai batter says he intends to stay true to his personality and lead on his own terms.

Pizza delivery turns fatal as Hyderabad man is shot multiple times in USAnshul Kuncha, 28, was working in an MNC in North Philadelphia, and would deliver pizzas on the weekends as a part-time job. He had delivered an order shortly before he was fired upon.

Easy to blame the actress: Janhvi Kapoor's make-up artist weighs in on Peddi rowThe debate around Janhvi Kapoor's role in Peddi has intensified after a member of her team shared claims about what happened behind the scenes. Here's what sparked the latest discussion.

IND vs AFG Highlights: Manav Suthar impresses on debutIndia (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) Only Test Highlights, Day 2: AFG (113/5 in 39.5 ovs). Manav Suthar impressed in his first outing with the ball, taking three wickets to give India the early breakthroughs to keep them well ahead at the end of the second day's play.

Zverev looks to break Slam curse, but Cobolli aims to spoil the partyFrench Open 2026: Alexander Zverev faces Flavio Cobolli in the Roland Garros men's singles final on Sunday in Paris. Zverev is chasing a first Grand Slam title while Cobolli seeks to complete a breakthrough run.

Godzilla El Nino could break records. How India's monsoon is in firing lineEl Nino has crossed the threshold in the Pacific, and a new forecast warns it could become the strongest ever recorded, just as India's monsoon has arrived late.

Why Indians are paying thousands to hug, cuddle cowsFor generations, Indians hugged, groomed and cared for cows without giving it a name. Now, tiny cows, curated sessions and wellness packages worth thousands of rupees, are powering a cow-cuddling boom in India. Cow farms to luxury retreats to spiritual centres like ISKCON, where it's free, cow cuddling is everywhere.
Bengaluru techie drives Rapido on weekends despite earning a six-figure salaryA Rapido ride in Bengaluru led to a conversation between a passenger and a software engineer who drives on weekends.

Back-to-back IPL titles, 0 caps: Rajat Patidar might never play T20Is for IndiaAt 33, Rajat Patidar led RCB to back-to-back IPL titles, yet was ignored for India's T20I reset. It explores the brutal bias of potential over maturity and his stoic, immortal legacy.

The next AI race is not about models. It is about rulesThe country that builds the cleverest machine will not inherit the next era of finance. The country that writes the rules those machines must obey will. India has been handed a rare seat at that table. It may be the last to understand what it is sitting at.

He kept kissing me: Actor Priya Bapat says she was harassed on set, felt helplessActor Priya Bapat recalled a co-actor repeatedly kissing her while improvising during a song sequence in her debut film. She said the experience left her distressed.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Jantar Mantar just a trailer: CJP's Abhijeet Dipke warns of bigger protestsA day after the Jantar Mantar demonstration, Abhijeet Dipke thanked supporters and called it a trailer for a bigger campaign. He gave Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan seven days to step down or face further on-ground protests.

El Nino effect: These cities will see dramatic changes in rain patternsA strong El Nino is building in the Pacific for June to August 2026, and the WMO expects it to reshape rainfall across the world's cities. From Delhi and Sydney to Johannesburg and the Ecuador coast, here is who faces drought and who faces downpours.

'Anti-tourist' summer and the quest for hidden gemsHandcrafted ByOur Editors Indian travellers are increasingly chasing offbeat escapes and "best-kept secrets" instead of overcrowded tourist hotspots. But in a world where every hidden gem is seemingly one viral post away from losing its charm, how are people still finding places that feel undiscovered?

Higher CTC, lower salary? Techie's Rs 36 LPA offer sparks online debateA Gurugram-based tech professional with 5.8 years of experience sparked online debate after revealing a confusing job offer. Despite a higher Rs 36 LPA CTC from a Mumbai role, the monthly in-hand salary drops slightly due to variable pay structure. The dilemma highlights how CTC doesn't always reflect actual take-home pay.

Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella says AI agents should be treated like employees, here is whySatya Nadella said companies should give AI agents identities, permissions and sandboxes. He said tighter governance and monitoring will be vital as agents take on bigger workplace tasks.

A crown, a campus and a Bulldog dream: Inside Drake University's unique pageantDrake University's annual Beautiful Bulldog Contest proves beauty pageants aren't just for humans. Celebrating its 47th year, the unique event crowns the most charming English Bulldog after a runway-style competition.

sports.headline: Shreyas Iyer's bold first words as India captain: Won't be under anyone's shadow Shreyas Iyer has embraced the challenge of becoming India's new T20I captain, but insists the leadership role will not change who he is. The Mumbai batter says he intends to stay true to his personality and lead on his own terms.

Group A preview: India face tricky test in three-way battle Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Group A features a high-stakes battle among six-time champions Australia, India and South Africa, alongside Pakistan, Bangladesh and debutants Netherlands.

Spinner Manav Suthar strikes in first over on Test debut India debutant Manav Suthar enjoyed a dream start to his Test career, claiming his maiden wicket in his very first over against Afghanistan. The left-arm spinner was India's standout bowler on Day 2 of the one-off Test in Mullanpur.

FIFA World Cup Group I Preview: Will Africa's elite outshine Mbappe vs Haaland Group I preview: World Cup heavyweights France and a historic Senegal side collision in a fascinating section, while an explosive Norway team and stubborn Iraq look to cause ultimate chaos.

Sooryavanshi ready for Test cricket: People know my T20 side, not red-ball Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has moved quickly to dismiss suggestions that he is only built for T20 cricket. The teenage batting sensation says Test cricket remains a major ambition and insists he has the game, temperament and experience to succeed across all three formats.

Praggnanandhaa is more dangerous than Magnus Carlsen: GM Thipsay Rameshbabu Praggnanandhaa is currently a more dangerous player than World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen, according to veteran Grandmaster Pravin Thipsay. Pragg recently beat Carlsen twice en route to win Norway Chess tournament.

PM Modi called, aim is now to go sub-10 seconds: Gurindervir Singh Sprinter Gurindervir Singh has revealed that he was left pleasantly surprised when Prime Minister Narendra Modi gave him a phone call after the former broke the 100m national record. Gurindervir became the fastest man in India after beating Animesh Kujur in the final of Federation Cup.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi says Virat Kohli chat felt like a dream For Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, IPL 2026 ended with more than just a record-breaking season. The 15-year-old got a personal pep talk from childhood idol Virat Kohli and admits the entire moment felt like a dream come true.

Iran accuse USA of holding back World Cup staff visas FIFA World Cup 2026: Iran say several team officials and support staff have been denied US visas before the World Cup. The row has widened concerns over host nation obligations and threatens to disrupt final preparations.

Portugal's Rafael Leao sent off for fistfight with Chile defender before WC FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal faces an anxious countdown as FIFA reviews Rafael Leao's violent conduct red card from the Chile friendly. A harsh ruling could shatter Roberto Martínez's tactical plans by sidelining the star winger for the crucial World Cup opener.

Clear edges, plumb LBW: Afghans miss out on 3 dismissals vs India in Mullanpur India vs Afghanistan: Shubman Gill and Rishabh Pant survived clear-cut dismissals as poor decision-making and poor umpiring hurt Afghanistan in the one-off Test match in Mullanpur. Despite bowling well in the morning session, Afghanistan were not able to pile up pressure on India.

Undesirable to undeniable: Shreyas Iyer's T20I remontada complete with India captaincyShreyas Iyer becomes the new India T20I captain, completing a turnaround that sees him re-enter the team for the first time since December 2023. Since then, Shreyas has reinvented himself as a modern T20 batter and a brave captain.

BCCI requests Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents to travel with Team IndiaThe Indian Cricket Board has made special provisions for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on tours of Sri Lanka and UK. The BCCI has asked Vaibhav's parents to travel with Team India as the 15-year-old assimilates into the national setup.

Zverev looks to break Slam curse, but Cobolli aims to spoil the partyFrench Open 2026: Alexander Zverev faces Flavio Cobolli in the Roland Garros men's singles final on Sunday in Paris. Zverev is chasing a first Grand Slam title while Cobolli seeks to complete a breakthrough run.

IND vs AFG Live: Manav Suthar impresses on debutIndia (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) Only Test Live Score and Updates, Day 2: AFG (113/5 in 39.5 ovs). Manav Suthar impressed in his first outing with the ball, taking three wickets to give India the early breakthroughs to keep them well ahead at the end of the second day's play.

Back-to-back IPL titles, 0 caps: Rajat Patidar might never play T20Is for IndiaAt 33, Rajat Patidar led RCB to back-to-back IPL titles, yet was ignored for India's T20I reset. It explores the brutal bias of potential over maturity and his stoic, immortal legacy.

Mirra Andreeva channels inner Roger Federer, flips switch to French Open gloryFrench Open 2026: Mirra Andreeva said she tried to draw inspiration from Roger Federer in the Roland Garros final against Maja Chwalinska. On Saturday, the 19-year-old captured her maiden Grand Slam title with a straight-sets win on Centre Court.

Shubman Gill advised to focus on WTC and ODI World Cup to avoid burnoutIndia's selection committee has asked Shubman Gill to prioritise Test and ODI cricket through the 2027 World Cup cycle, aiming to manage his workload and prevent burnout despite his strong IPL 2026 season.

India outclass Bangladesh to win SAFF Women's Championship after seven yearsIndia defeated Bangladesh 3-1 in the SAFF Women's Championship final to clinch a record-extending sixth title, ending a seven-year wait and completing an unbeaten campaign.

USA detains Iraq football team's striker, interrogates him for 7 hoursIraq's World Cup striker Aymen Hussein was held and questioned for nearly seven hours at Chicago's O'Hare Airport. The move has once again put the spotlight on an issue that fans and critics had expressed concerns about in the lead-up to the tournament.

Nothing's come easy for Suryakumar Yadav: Rohit Sharma reacts after captaincy sackRohit Sharma backed Suryakumar Yadav after his T20I axing, praising his resilience and journey to the top, while also endorsing Shreyas Iyer as India's new T20 captain.

Afghanistan spinners struggled, but head coach sees valuable lessonsRichard Pybus said Afghanistan's inexperienced spinners will learn from a bruising day against India in Mullanpur. He pointed to Kharote's character, Malik's no-balls and Rahul's reprieve as key themes of the day.

Mirra Andreeva lifts French Open trophy twice in funny goof-up: WatchFrench Open 2026: Fresh from winning her maiden Roland Garros title, Mirra Andreeva amused fans with a post-match trophy-lifting mix-up. On Saturday, the 19-year-old beat Maja Chwalinska to claim her maiden Grand Slam title.

Sooryavanshi's childhood coach on India call-up: BCCI have found their poster boyVaibhav Sooryavanshi's childhood coach, Manish Ojha, hailed the 15-year-old's maiden India call-up, saying the BCCI may have found its next poster boy. Congratulating the youngster on his historic selection, Ojha expressed hope that Sooryavanshi would become one of the faces of Indian cricket in the years to come.

Mirra Andreeva ends Maja Chwalinska's dream run, wins maiden Grand Slam titleFrench Open 2026: Mirra Andreeva fulfilled her Grand Slam dream at Roland Garros, defeating Maja Chwalinska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest women's singles champion in Paris since Monica Seles in 1992 and claim her maiden major title.

R Praggnanandhaa an incredible fighter: Carlsen praises Norway Chess title winMagnus Carlsen praised R Praggnanandhaa after the Indian Grandmaster made history as the first player from India to win the Norway Chess title.

Ex-Mumbai cricketer among 25 others provisionally suspended for dopingFormer Mumbai cricketer Bhupen Lalwani is among 25 athletes provisionally suspended by NADA for doping offences, with national record holder KM Deeksha also featuring on the list.

Suryakumar Yadav reacts to India's T20I captaincy change: Happy for Shreyas IyerIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav has sent his best wishes to Shreyas Iyer after the Indian selectors named the latter captain of the T20I side. The Ajit Agarkar-led selection panel handed the baton to Shreyas, who has not played a T20I since December 2023.

50 in 50: Rishabh Pant takes out IPL's anger on Abdul MalikIndia wicketkeeper-batter Rishabh Pant looked in good touch on his return to the Test set-up. Pant scored a fifty in 70 balls in his 50th Test match and remained unbeaten at the end of Day 1 in the only Test match against Afghanistan.

Gambhir's faith gave me freedom to perform: Sai credits India coach for 81 vs AFGSai Sudharsan credited head coach Gautam Gambhir's unwavering backing for giving him the freedom and mental clarity to express himself at the crease after scoring a fluent 81 on the opening day of India's one-off Test against Afghanistan.

Suryakumar will leave behind an MS Dhoni-like legacy as India captain: GavaskarFormer India captain Sunil Gavaskar believes Suryakumar Yadav will leave behind a captaincy legacy similar to that of MS Dhoni, despite losing both the T20I captaincy and his place in India's squad just three months after leading the team to a successful T20 World Cup title defence.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
